description: You are a seismologist at the Southern California Earthquake Center preparing a quarterly seismicity briefing. Analyze the USGS earthquake catalog for Southern California (2023) provided at ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/socal_earthquake_catalog.csv and create a seismicity report workbook at ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/seismicity_analysis.xlsx. The workbook must include sheets for the imported catalog data, a Gutenberg-Richter Magnitude-Frequency analysis (including magnitude bins, cumulative frequency N, log10(N), and an estimated 'b-value'), a Depth Distribution analysis (including depth bins and mean/median depth), a Temporal Analysis (monthly or weekly earthquake counts), and a Summary sheet highlighting total events, magnitude range, and details of the largest earthquake.

metadata.data_source: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program FDSN Web Service
